response:
Short version
- azr-compliance-v3 supports both per‑subscription (targeted) and bulk “ensure Event Grid” remediation for subscriptions that are in Rackspace‑managed monitoring scope but missing the required Event Grid subscription.
- Inventory is first scoped and filtered to Rackspace‑managed monitoring subscriptions (lighthouse_delegations join + name/destination ownership rules) so remediation only acts on subscriptions Rackspace owns/should manage.

How a remediation request runs (end‑to‑end)
1. Frontend
   - The EventGridDetails UI shows explicit buckets (Ready For Event Grid Deployment vs Monitoring Required First) and exposes per‑subscription Ensure Event Grid and Ensure All Deployable (bulk) actions.
   - For bulk remediation the page issues one POST /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk and then polls GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest to merge per‑item statuses into the visible cards and show a Latest Bulk Remediation panel.
   - For targeted remediation the frontend calls the backend endpoint POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure (earlier experiments tried calling Fleet directly from the browser, but the deployed flow uses the backend).

2. Backend orchestration / Fleet/APIM call
   - The backend receives the request and calls Fleet’s Event Grid management endpoint via APIM:
     https://…/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription
   - The backend acquires an authorization token for APIM. Historically there were a few variants in the repo:
     - initial browser‑side calls used the operator MSAL token,
     - then a ClientSecretCredential flow was used to get the application token for api://…/.default,
     - later the implementation was changed to reuse the Rackspace identity flow (shared.janus_helpers.get_rackspace_identity_token) and send that token to APIM.
   - After calling APIM the backend polls the returned statusUrl until the orchestration reaches a terminal runtimeStatus.

3. Status semantics and error mapping
   - The poll treats succeeded and already_exists as success.
   - HTTP 404 from Fleet/APIM is mapped to not_found; 409 to not_eligible (and other mappings are applied to present operator‑friendly statuses).
   - Targeted delete operations validate that the subscription belongs to the requested customer and emit a Cosmos event on a customer/subscription mismatch before failing.

Bulk remediation implementation details
- Bulk remediation is not shoved through the old sync_queue. Instead it uses Postgres tables event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items and a dedicated timer worker ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation.
- The worker runs on a 15‑second timer and is guarded by its own advisory lock so only one instance processes a run at a time.
- Starting a bulk run snapshots the full set of deployable subscriptions from the shared Event Grid coverage SQL and persists per‑item status so UI page refreshes can continue to show progress.
- The frontend starts a run once and polls the latest run row; the backend updates run_items as per‑subscription actions progress.

Inventory & safety controls
- SyncEventGrid only surfaces Rackspace‑managed monitoring subscriptions (joins to lighthouse_delegations with managed_by_tenant_id == Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and allowed delegation states).
- Sync filters out unmanaged Event Grid rows by name and destination patterns (Rackspace naming conventions, trusted webhook/resource hosts), and reports skipped/unmanaged counts in sync stats.
- APIM and backend policy fixes were applied in prod so the Fleet/APIM audience and path rewrites work reliably.

In short: the UI drives targeted or bulk Ensure Event Grid actions -> backend mediates calls to Fleet via APIM using a Rackspace identity token -> backend polls Fleet’s statusUrl and maps outcomes -> bulk runs are persisted in Postgres and executed by a locked 15s timer worker so progress can be reported and retried safely.
context_injected:
--- Episode 1 [codex 2026-04-09] (similarity: 0.7211) ---
Implemented dedicated server-side Event Grid bulk remediation in azr-compliance-v3. Design choice: do not reuse sync_queue; instead use Postgres-backed tables event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items plus a dedicated 15-second timer worker ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation guarded by its own advisory lock. Bulk start endpoint reuses an active queued/running run, snapshots the full deployable subscription set from shared Event Grid coverage SQL, and persists latest-run item statuses for page refresh continuity. EventGridDetails now calls POST /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk once, polls GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest, merges latest item statuses into visible deployable cards, and shows a Latest Bulk Remediation panel.

--- Episode 2 [codex 2026-04-09] (similarity: 0.713) ---
On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, integrated Event Grid remediation on the Event Grid Details page as a browser-side Fleet Platform call rather than a backend proxy because the browser reliably has the operator MSAL Bearer token while the backend, behind APIM, may not retain the raw Authorization credential in deployed environments. The page now uses metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi), requests an optional Fleet-specific scope via VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE, calls POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription against VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_BASE_URL, polls statusUrl until terminal runtimeStatus, treats succeeded/already_exists as success, maps 404 to not_found, and maps 409 to not_eligible. UI adds per-subscription Ensure Event Grid and Ensure All Deployable actions plus local remediation state badges.

--- Episode 3 [codex 2026-04-09] (similarity: 0.7099) ---
azr-compliance-v3 Event Grid remediation was switched from a browser-side Fleet API call to a backend-mediated flow because Fleet requires application permission. The backend now acquires a ClientSecretCredential token for scope api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f/.default and calls https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription, polling the returned statusUrl until completion. The frontend calls POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure instead of Fleet directly.

--- Episode 4 [codex 2026-04-09] (similarity: 0.6672) ---
On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, redesigned Event Grid Details to distinguish deployment inventory from remediation coverage. GetEventGridDeployments now returns metadata.coverage with deployable_missing_event_grid_count, missing_monitoring_count, and arrays for deployable_subscriptions vs out_of_scope_subscriptions, derived from active customer subscriptions, monitoring entitlement, and Rackspace-managed Event Grid inventory. The frontend EventGridDetails page now shows explicit remediation buckets: 'Ready For Event Grid Deployment' for monitoring-enabled subscriptions missing Event Grid, and 'Monitoring Required First' for subscriptions outside monitoring scope. This resolves ambiguity in the prior 'Subscriptions Without Event Grid' card, which only reflected monitoring-entitled scope.

--- Episode 5 [codex 2026-04-09] (similarity: 0.6283) ---
azr-compliance-v3 Fleet Event Grid remediation token acquisition was revised again on 2026-04-09: instead of Entra client credentials, backend-py now reuses the existing Rackspace Identity flow backed by RACKSPACE_USERNAME and RACKSPACE_PASSWORD. shared/fleet_event_grid_helpers.py calls shared.janus_helpers.get_rackspace_identity_token(auth_mode='identity') and sends that token as Authorization: Bearer to APIM for the targeted Event Grid ensure endpoint.

--- Episode 6 [codex 2026-03-23] (similarity: 0.6225) ---
On 2026-03-23 in azr-fleet-platform PR #67, three additional Event Grid review findings were validated and fixed in commit 5cd48c3. Fixes: targeted Event Grid DELETE now validates that the subscription belongs to the URL customer before starting the delete orchestration while still skipping monitoring-tier eligibility; ensure_event_subscription_single now writes a Cosmos EventGridSubscriptionCustomerMismatch event before returning a customer/subscription mismatch failure; orchestrate_onboarding changed event_grid_result['skipped'] back to a boolean and added skipped_no_monitoring as the explicit count, with finalize_run updated to carry both fields. Targeted Event Grid tests passed and all new review threads were replied to and resolved.

--- Episode 7 [codex 2026-03-23] (similarity: 0.6023) ---
On 2026-03-23 in fleet-platform-app, targeted Event Grid remediation API failures in prod had two independent causes. First, azr-fleet-platform-prod was missing AUTH_TENANT_ID and AUTH_ALLOWED_AUDIENCES, causing backend function auth to return 'JWT validation is not configured; AUTH_TENANT_ID/AZURE_TENANT_ID missing' and 503 Authentication service unavailable for http_eventgrid_subscription_targeted. Fix: set AUTH_TENANT_ID=570057f4-73ef-41c8-bcbb-08db2fc15c2b and AUTH_ALLOWED_AUDIENCES=api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f,3eabce94-dd29-4358-b262-09a43f2ddb60 on azr-fleet-platform-prod and update deploy workflows to keep them. Second, the public APIM route still failed even after auth was fixed because agent-fleet-api APIM policy validated only old audience api://agent-fleet-platform and rewrote targeted Event Grid management paths to backend /api/customers/... instead of /api/v1/customers/.... ApiManagementGatewayLogs confirmed 404 backend URL https://azr-fleet-platform-prod.../api/customers/{ddi}/subscriptions/{sub}/eventgrid/subscription for correlation ids dc96056e-e73b-4775-9f1d-7e9c54d0c2cc and 6cd9e891-3f89-4fa1-b3cc-9841e3f39349. Repo fix is to add {{api-audience}} as JWT audience in global-policy*.xml and preserve /v1 for /v1/customers/.../eventgrid/... paths in APIM rewrite logic, plus maintain api-audience named value in deploy workflows.

--- Episode 8 [codex 2026-04-09] (similarity: 0.6018) ---
For azr-compliance-v3 PR #72 on 2026-04-09, two live GitHub review threads on GetEventGridDeployments were valid and fixed in commit 633573dc on develop. Fixes: (1) summary query no longer prepends an extra WITH before EVENT_GRID_EFFECTIVE_SCOPE_CTE, preventing invalid SQL 'WITH WITH ...'; (2) selected tenant_id now uses COALESCE(eegd.tenant_id, mms.tenant_id, cs.tenant_id) to match join/filter fallback behavior. Added unit assertions to catch both regressions, reran ruff and focused pytest, replied to both threads, and resolved them.

--- Episode 9 [codex 2026-04-08] (similarity: 0.5962) ---
On 2026-04-08 in azr-compliance-v3, implemented Event Grid inventory scoping and Rackspace ownership filtering. SyncEventGrid no longer scopes by customer_subscriptions + monitoring service blocks alone; it now scopes to managed monitoring subscriptions by joining customer_subscriptions to lighthouse_delegations, requiring managed_by_tenant_id to equal the Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state in active/succeeded. The sync still uses the single Rackspace Lighthouse credential and intentionally does not acquire per-customer tenant tokens. Event Grid rows are now filtered before upsert by a shared ownership classifier: event subscription name must match Rackspace naming conventions (rax-/rackspace-) and destination must match Rackspace-managed webhook/resource patterns (rackspace.com, compliance-v3 azurewebsites hosts, or known azr-fleet/compliance resource IDs). Sync stats now distinguish skipped unmanaged subscriptions, stale/missing subscriptions from SubscriptionNotFound warnings, and filtered unmanaged deployments. GetEventGridDeployments summary and list queries now use the same managed monitoring scope so page KPIs reflect only Rackspace-managed monitoring subscriptions and hide old out-of-scope inventory.

--- Episode 10 [codex 2026-04-08] (similarity: 0.5778) ---
On 2026-04-08 in azr-compliance-v3 planning, live Azure inspection showed the shared edge for governance/dev-governance is not codified in repo IaC. Application Gateway azr-waf-platform in resource group azr-fleet-platform-infra has one global WAF policy waf-apim attached (forceFirewallPolicyAssociation=true), shared listeners for governance/dev-governance/APIM/webhook, and path maps governance-path/dev-governance-path routing /policy-engine/v2/* to APIM pools and /* to frontend pools. WAF policy waf-apim is in Prevention mode with managed rule sets OWASP 3.2, Microsoft_BotManagerRuleSet 1.1, Microsoft_HTTPDDoSRuleSet 1.0, six RequestArgNames exclusions, and 13 enabled custom rules including BlockGovernanceUnexpectedPaths and BlockDevGovernanceUnexpectedPaths whose exact-route allowlists include /event-grid-details. Repo infrastructure currently deploys only env-scoped app resources via infrastructure/main-ase.bicep into dev-azr-compliance-v3 or prod-azr-compliance-v3; no Bicep for shared App Gateway/WAF exists. Planning decision from user: codify Gateway + WAF in this repo infrastructure and add drift detection/blocking.
